required_inputs: request intent and requested campaign trafficking action
lane scope and adjacent authority map
tenant approval, legal, editorial, creative, privacy, account, and contractual authority rules
refusal_triggers: request asks for legal, editorial, creative, privacy, media strategy, budget, targeting, financial, contractual, or final approval authority
request asks the lane to mark setup, launch, delivery, or closeout complete without evidence
request requires client commitment, account-management, policy-override, makegood, credit, or final acceptance authority

required_inputs: authoritative public source list
campaign channel, platform, publisher, ad format, creative ID, measurement, privacy, and workflow context
source recency and decay metadata
refusal_triggers: required channel, platform, publisher, ad-format, creative-ID, or source context is missing
public source, tenant policy, publisher policy, IO terms, platform specs, or client instruction materially conflict
source freshness no longer supports a confident campaign trafficking workflow result

required_inputs: advertiser, campaign, placement, line item, flight, channel, publisher, platform, and ad server identifiers
IO, campaign brief, media plan, trafficking, creative, creative ID, tag, pixel, UTM, URL, QA, launch, delivery, approval, and closeout records
completion and delivery evidence
refusal_triggers: campaign brief, IO, creative approval, creative ID, trafficking sheet, tag, pixel, URL, QA, launch, delivery, approval, or proof evidence is incomplete or unverifiable
source records conflict with ad server, publisher, verification, client, or approval records
requested completion is not supported by traceable source evidence

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory
record-location and retrieval hooks
owner, reviewer, approver, publisher, ad server, QA, trafficking, and client-delivery map
required_outputs: blocker summary with missing systems or records
refusal_triggers: required system of record is unavailable
client authority, campaign owner, approval owner, trafficker, publisher owner, or ad server owner is unknown
campaign, creative, ad server, trafficking, verification, delivery, project, CRM, ticketing, document, approval, or client-delivery source path cannot be retrieved

required_inputs: missing-fact flags
confidence rubric
authority, source-conflict, confidentiality, approval, delivery, and client-impact indicators
refusal_triggers: critical tenant fact, advertiser, campaign scope, channel, platform, approval rule, authority source, or system record is unknown
conflicting public guidance, tenant policy, publisher policy, IO terms, platform rules, or client instructions remain unresolved
required approvals are missing for the requested trafficking workflow action

required_inputs: scope outcome
adjacent specialist registry
handoff artifact requirements
required_outputs: handoff package with owner and missing dependencies
refusal_triggers: request is outside campaign trafficking intake, setup, creative and tag handling, tracking QA, launch evidence, delivery monitoring, exception handling, or completion evidence capture
request requires legal, editorial, creative approval, media strategy, privacy, account, finance, contractual, client-rights, quality, delivery, or final approval authority
request requires formal judgment instead of evidence-backed operational handling

required_inputs: accepted work item and target lifecycle state
required source records, approvals, QA gates, due dates, and rollback state
tenant state model and completion gates
refusal_triggers: requested state transition lacks required prerequisites
workflow is blocked by missing campaign, creative, creative ID, trafficking, tracking, QA, approval, launch, delivery, or proof evidence
requested transition would bypass legal, editorial, creative, privacy, account, publisher, platform, quality, or client approval

required_inputs: exception category and affected campaign trafficking state
campaign, creative, creative ID, tag, pixel, URL, publisher, platform, delivery, discrepancy, approval, or confidentiality blocker
owner, due date, and escalation threshold
refusal_triggers: exception cannot be mapped to an owner or rule class
tag failure, publisher rejection, tracking mismatch, delivery discrepancy, source conflict, confidentiality issue, or approval issue remains unresolved
the request asks to suppress the exception to force launch or completion

required_inputs: completion target and evidence checklist
setup record, tag validation, pixel proof, QA evidence, launch proof, screenshots, delivery logs, proof of performance, approval references, and retention location
tenant completion and audit logging rules
refusal_triggers: setup, tag, pixel, launch, delivery, closeout, QA, screenshot, proof, approval, or retention evidence is missing
client delivery record, owner approval, or required proof of performance is absent
requested completion would misstate actual documented workflow state

required_inputs: client engagement scope and confidentiality records
audience-data, campaign-data, creative-asset, tag-sheet, credential, and approved-channel requirements
client commitments, delivery obligations, due dates, and approval constraints
refusal_triggers: request would disclose or reuse client files, audience data, platform credentials, unreleased creative, tag sheets, campaign setup, or performance data without approved scope
requested channel or recipient is not approved
client commitment, IO term, engagement scope, or delivery obligation conflicts with legal, privacy, policy, or confidentiality rules

required_inputs: requested professional, legal, editorial, creative, financial, or contractual action
legal, editorial, privacy, creative approval, delivery, account, finance, media strategy, and client approver map
engagement, IO, policy, and approval boundaries
refusal_triggers: request requires legal interpretation, claim substantiation, editorial approval, creative approval, privacy-law interpretation, budget approval, makegood approval, credit approval, final acceptance, or contractual signoff
request changes client commitments, IO obligations, account terms, delivery obligations, or financial exposure
client rights, privacy obligations, regulated content, or professional judgment determine the answer
